dynamic_line: Welcome to the supply depot. What do you need?
I'm responsible for all equipment and supplies here. State your business.
If you're looking for gear, you've come to the right place. Though supplies are limited these days.
Everything that comes in and goes out passes through me. What can I help you with?

responses.text: What kind of supplies do you have?
Do you need any specific items?
I'll leave you to your work.

dynamic_line: Warrant Officer Class 2 Jenkins, Royal Logistics Corps. Been managing military supplies for twenty years.
Staff Sergeant Thompson, quartermaster for this unit. I keep track of everything that comes in and goes out.
Sergeant Major Wilson. I'm in charge of supplies, equipment, and making sure nothing walks off without authorization.
Colour Sergeant Davies. I've been keeping the lads equipped since before most of them were in nappies.
Not good. We're running low on everything. No resupply since this all started.
Critical items are being rationed. Ammunition, medical supplies, food - all in short supply.
We're making do with what we have, but it won't last forever. Been salvaging what we can from abandoned positions.
Could be worse. We managed to secure this depot before things went completely pear-shaped. But supplies are dwindling.
Standard military kit. Uniforms, webbing, some weapons and ammunition. All strictly rationed, mind you.
Basic equipment for our personnel. Nothing fancy, but it keeps them operational.
We've got clothing, some weapons, basic field equipment. Medical supplies are prioritized for the field hospital.
Whatever was left when the supply chain collapsed. Some ammunition, uniforms, field rations. Not enough of anything.

responses.text: How do you manage inventory with limited supplies?

dynamic_line: Strict accounting. Everything is logged, prioritized, and distributed based on operational needs.
Carefully. Essential personnel get priority. Everyone else makes do with what's left.
I've implemented a voucher system. Each unit gets an allocation based on their mission requirements.
With difficulty. I keep track of everything on paper now. Old school, but reliable when the computers went down.
Ammunition is our biggest concern. 5.56mm for the L85s especially. Can never have enough of that.
Medical supplies, batteries, fuel if you can find it. Anything that helps keep us operational.
Tools and repair materials. Need to maintain what equipment we have since replacements aren't coming.
Food with long shelf life. The ration packs won't last forever, and we've got hungry soldiers to feed.

responses.text: I'll keep an eye out for those items.

dynamic_line: Much appreciated. Bring anything useful you find, and I'll make sure it gets to where it's needed most.
That would be helpful. We're trying to establish some self-sufficiency, but certain items can't be improvised.
Good. If you do find anything, I'll make sure you're compensated fairly. We take care of those who help us.
Thank you. Every bit helps in keeping these lads equipped and fighting fit.
